sections.content:

You are still on the same level.

Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Agent at [10,22] in blue start zone. White passages map revealed: rows 14-18 cols 20-24 (left), rows 14-18 cols 32-38 (right), rows 19-25 cols 32-38, rows 26-38 cols 20-38 (wide), rows 42-43 cols 20-63 (bottom). End zone at [52,46]. Red path traces solution route along right side then bottom.
- Goal model: Navigate agent through white passages to reach end zone.
- Action model: Movement is 6 cells per action. RIGHT/DOWN blocked from current position.
- Recent findings: Full maze structure mapped. Agent stuck - need to find way down from start zone.
- Open questions: Does the maze scroll or does the agent move through it? What's the actual path?
- Plan: Go LEFT back to [10,16], then try DOWN. If blocked, need to find another route.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Excellent! The agent moved! After LEFT then DOWN:
- LEFT: Agent moved from [10, 22] to [10, 16]
- DOWN: Agent moved from [10, 16] to [16, 22] (the boundary shows [[14, 20], [14, 24], [18, 24], [18, 20]], which is a 5x5 area centered at [16, 22])
Wait, the boundary is [[14, 20], [14, 24], [18, 24], [18, 20]], which means the agent is now in a 5x5 area at rows 14-18, cols 20-24. This is the left passage!
So the agent successfully moved DOWN from [10, 16] to [16, 22]. The start zone moved with the agent.
But wait, earlier when the agent was at [10, 22], DOWN was blocked. But when the agent was at [10, 16], DOWN worked. This means the wall at row 13 doesn't span the entire width - there's an opening at cols 14-18 (the original start zone position).
So the maze structure is:
- Start zone at rows 8-12, cols 14-18 (original)
- Opening in wall at row 13, cols 14-18
- Left passage at rows 14-18, cols 20-24
Wait, but the agent moved from [10, 16] to [16, 22]. This is a movement of +6 rows and +6 columns. So the agent moved diagonally?
No, wait. The agent moved LEFT from [10, 22] to [10, 16] (-6 columns). Then DOWN from [10, 16] to [16, 22] (+6 rows, +6 columns).
Hmm, that's strange. The DOWN movement resulted in +6 rows AND +6 columns. So the agent moved diagonally?
Actually, looking at the boundary [[14, 20], [14, 24], [18, 24], [18, 20]], the agent is now at rows 14-18, cols 20-24. The center is [16, 22].
So from [10, 16], the agent moved DOWN to [16, 22]. This is +6 rows and +6 columns. So the agent moved diagonally down-right?
Wait, that doesn't make sense. Let me reconsider.
Actually, I think the issue is that the start zone follows the agent. When the agent moved LEFT from [10, 22] to [10, 16], the start zone moved from cols 20-24 to cols 14-18. When the agent moved DOWN from [10, 16], the start zone moved from rows 8-12 to rows 14-18, and also from cols 14-18 to cols 20-24.
Wait, that's strange. The start zone moved both down AND right. This suggests that the agent moved diagonally.
But the action was DOWN, not DOWN-RIGHT. So why did the agent move diagonally?
Hmm, maybe the maze structure is such that the only valid DOWN movement from [10, 16] is to [16, 22]. The white passage at rows 14-18, cols 20-24 is the only valid destination for a DOWN movement.
Actually, I think the issue is that the start zone is a 5x5 area, and the agent is at the center. When the agent moves DOWN, the start zone moves to the next 5x5 room in the DOWN direction. The next 5x5 room in the DOWN direction from rows 8-12, cols 14-18 is rows 14-18, cols 20-24 (the left passage).
So the movement is not simply 6 cells in the direction. The agent moves to the center of the next room in that direction. The rooms are connected, and the agent moves between them.
OK, so the agent is now at [16, 22], which is in the left passage (rows 14-18, cols 20-24). From here, the agent can:
- RIGHT: Move to the right passage (rows 14-18, cols 32-38)
- DOWN: Move to... where? The white passages at rows 19-25 are only at cols 32-38 (right passage), not at cols 20-24 (left passage).
- LEFT: Move back to the start zone
- UP: Move back to the start zone
So from the left passage, the agent can only move RIGHT to the right passage. Let me try RIGHT.
Actually, looking at the white passage map:
- Rows 14-18: cols 20-24 (left), cols 32-38 (right)
- Rows 19-25: cols 32-38 (right only)
- Rows 26-38: cols 20-38 (wide)
- Rows 42-43: cols 20-63 (bottom)
So the path is:
1. Start zone -> left passage (rows 14-18, cols 20-24)
2. Left passage -> right passage (rows 14-18, cols 32-38)
3. Right passage -> right passage continues (rows 19-25, cols 32-38)
4. Right passage continues -> wide passage (rows 26-38, cols 20-38)
5. Wide passage -> bottom passage (rows 42-43, cols 20-63)
6. Bottom passage -> end zone (rows 49-55, cols 43-49)
From the current position [16, 22] (left passage), the agent needs to move RIGHT to the right passage.
Let me try RIGHT.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Agent at [16,22] in left passage (rows 14-18, cols 20-24). DOWN from [10,16] worked, moving agent to [16,22] (diagonal +6,+6). Start zone follows agent. Path: left passage → right passage → down → wide passage → bottom → end zone.
Goal model: Navigate through white passages to end zone at [52,46].
Action model: Agent moves to center of next connected room in direction. Movement is 6 cells.
Recent findings: Agent successfully entered left passage. Need to continue through maze.
Plan: Move RIGHT to right passage, then DOWN through the maze to the end zone.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# From left passage [16,22], move RIGHT to right passage
action(['RIGHT'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
agent_node = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['id']==8][0]
print(f"Agent after RIGHT: boundary={agent_node['boundary']}")

# Check if level completed
print(f"Result: {last_action_result}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
